Transcrição Completa: 4 Video Pitches with Michael Supina   [00:00]  "All right. What's going on, guys? So, Jeremy kind of asked you guys, but who in here does video pitches consistently? Consistent. Consistently, right? Who in here does at least 200 video pitches a month? Who in here does at least 500 video pitches a month? Who in here does at least a thousand video pitches?"   [00:25]  "I know Tom does. Tom does a little bit of the same strategy here. So our agency acquisition machine consists of four VAs, one salesperson and I am not involved anymore. Right? So myself as a business owner does not need to be involved in any part of this process anymore to be able to get out a thousand video pitches. Right?"   [00:48]  "Today I'm going to show you how to set it up from A to Z so you guys can stop being poor, right? I'm still a little bit poor when you're in a room with this guy. So, you know..."   [01:02]  "So just to be clear, Jeremy's voice here. This machine can be used for any service-based business. I personally don't think that this is subjected to just agencies. So, if you don't own an agency, you have some kind of service base, this will still work as long as you can create a video pitch."   [01:20]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Video Pitch Process Overview"   NÚMEROS:  4 VAs, 1 Salesperson, 1,000 video pitches   VISUAL:  Diagrama de fluxo mostrando o processo completo da máquina de aquisição de agências, com setas conectando diferentes etapas do processo   [01:25]  "I think video pitches are great, as Jeremy said, for almost any kind of business that's providing some type of service. So, this is our process. I'm going to go through basically like each step right here. And then we'll break down each step in different slides, right?"   [01:42]  "So, it's going to be fairly tactical. This is not going to be like a hurrah, right? It's going to be like very like this is how you set it up. This is who's involved. So you guys can start doing basically the same thing."   [01:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Step 1: Hunters"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Ícone de caçador ou lupa, representando a busca de leads, primeira etapa do processo destacada   [01:58]  "So what we have first here, we have what we call our hunters, right? So you got to have somebody that goes and finds the leads, right? So you can use different softwares and stuff like that, but somebody has to actually go physically find these leads."   [02:15]  "So I was talking to somebody earlier, I think I forgot who it was. We're talking about like possibly using AI to filter leads and find leads for you would be awesome, right?"   [02:25]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Step 2: Lead Grading"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Sistema de classificação visual, possível escala ou níveis de qualificação de leads   [02:28]  "The next thing they do is they just add it to one big Google sheet and then it goes over to somebody who actually grades these leads. So, I don't know if you guys work with VAs often, but VAs typically aren't as critically thinking as we are, right?"   [02:43]  "They don't know exactly what you want. You kind of have to give them something very specific to go after. Kind of give them a set of rules and you need somebody who can actually grade the leads at a different level, right?"   [02:55]  "Because you as an advertiser, and you guys can correct me if I'm wrong, but when you look at a client, how many of us can tell like it's a baller client or it's a shitty client, right? How many of us can teach somebody to understand who doesn't advertise if it's a good or bad client? It's a little bit more difficult, right?"   [03:15]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Step 3: Video Editor"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Ícone de edição de vídeo, software Camtasia mencionado   [03:18]  "So, you're going to have somebody who grades. Then it goes over to your video editor, right? So, you need somebody who obviously edits these videos. This is going to be another VA. We edit these in Camtasia."   [03:30]  "Pretty much a lot of the stuff that Jeremy done, I basically have just somewhat put it on steroids, if you will."   [03:37]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Step 4: Sniper (Contact Finder)"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Ícone de alvo ou mira, representando precisão na busca de contatos   [03:40]  "Next thing we go over to our sniper. So the sniper here is the head hunter, right? It's the person who basically finds the contacts for the businesses or the companies, right? So he's going out and finding every single contact. Just one person's able to do that."   [03:58]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Step 5: Lemlist Upload & Salesperson"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Logo ou interface do Lemlist, conexão com salesperson   [04:02]  "Then he adds it to another sheet. That sheet then is uploaded into a software that we'll go over here called Lemlist. And then it goes to the salesperson, right?"   [04:12]  "So the software Lemlist is really like the juice in this whole thing that makes it possible to even reach out to like a thousand people."   [04:20]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Hunters - Lead Finding"   NÚMEROS:  2-3 VAs   VISUAL:  Múltiplos ícones de pessoas representando VAs, fontes de leads listadas   [04:25]  "First you've got your hunters, right? So, your hunters find the leads. So, you're going to have probably anywhere between two to three different VAs doing this, and you're going to be finding your virtual assistants on online jobs.ph."   [04:42]  "If you guys have ever used that website, online jobs, what we have actually done is we found one from the beginning, and one was like my OG, right? It's been like three years since I had this guy and he now is like a recruiter of VAs for me."   [04:58]  "I have his wife on staff. I have his wife's brother on staff. I have like two of his friends on staff now. Like this guy recruits everybody for me. So if you find a good VA, you don't have to continue to do this and he can be like the manager itself, right?"   [05:15]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lead Sources"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Lista de plataformas - Google, Amazon, Instagram, storeleads.com   [05:20]  "So the lead hunters, they're going to be using different types of locations, different like websites and stuff. So, one of them obviously is like Google. Depending on what you guys do, this is going to be mostly e-commerce."   [05:35]  "So, if you're in personal brands, you guys are going to be able to use like Instagram to find these types of people and stuff like that, right? So, we use Google, Amazon. Amazon, you're capable of actually finding different leads. You can type in different keywords, right?"   [05:52]  "We use a website called storeleads.com. So, if anybody does e-commerce, look at storeleads.com. That one will give you a list of so many different e-commerce websites."   [06:05]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lead Grading Criteria - The Challenge"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Diagrama de decisão ou flowchart para qualificação de leads   [06:08]  "And then from there, the hardest part, as I mentioned, is actually grading, giving them a criteria for these leads, right? So, especially in e-commerce, you're probably going to come across like a bunch of shit leads, right?"   [06:22]  "Like a bunch of like shitty stores that make no money, right? How many of you guys have seen like all sorts of stores, they literally don't make any money? Have you hopped on sales? Have you guys hopped on sales calls with stores that make no money?"   [06:35]  "Right? It's tough. So what you want to do is you really want to make sure that your team is capable of at least filtering these to an extent so that you guys don't have to waste time on sales calls. Your sales guy doesn't waste any time. Right?"   [06:50]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lead Grading Structure"   NÚMEROS:  Sistema de 4 cores - Verde, Vermelho, Laranja, Amarelo   VISUAL:  Mapa de classificação com cores, critérios de avaliação incluindo qualidade do website e unicidade do produto   [06:55]  "So this is kind of like my very janky structure here that I built for them. Basically the first off is like is the website shitty or not? Right? You guys would be surprised how often my VAs give me just like somebody who I'm like bro this website looks so bad no one's buying anything from it. Right?"   [07:15]  "Then you just filter through, is the product unique? And then basically what we have is we grade them green, red, orange, yellow. So you can see all like kind of the grading."   [07:25]  "So make sure if you're going to give this to a VA that the VA has some type of grading map here that they're able to actually like kind of refer back to, but then you're going to still have somebody that grades them, right?"   [07:38]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Individual VA Sheets"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Múltiplas planilhas Google Sheets, sistema de rastreamento individual   [07:42]  "So then they add them to a sheet. And Jay, I think most of this stuff got cut off. I don't know where you're at, but most like these are added to a sheet. Each one has their own sheet. So we can kind of look at basically who has been able to bring us what."   [07:58]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lead Grader - Critical Role"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Posição destacada do Lead Grader no processo, conexão com expertise de marketing   [08:02]  "Then you go into like the lead grader. So this is what I talk about somebody who actually understands the business itself. So I personally believe that in all of this you should do every single piece of this first. Right?"   [08:18]  "If you just try to get somebody else to do it, you're going to struggle with them actually like doing it right. You're going to sit back. It's going to be done wrong. You're going to wonder why you don't have any booked calls."   [08:30]  "So you got to do all this stuff first. So you need to go through the leads. You need to take the calls. You need to do everything right."   [08:38]  "So lead grader is your salesperson or a current marketer. So whoever is actually currently doing the advertising is really good to have actually grade these leads cuz they understand who to actually put into your system."   [08:52]  "Cuz you got to understand if this isn't right, the rest of the system is screwed up, right? All the money you're putting into it, if the calls come out on the other end shitty, you're wasting everybody's time. So this is a really important step to make sure that all the leads you have are completely graded."   [09:10]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "4-Level Grading System"   NÚMEROS:  4 níveis - Vermelho, Amarelo, Laranja, Verde   VISUAL:  Sistema de classificação por cores em ordem crescente de qualidade   [09:15]  "So, we have four levels of grading. Right? Lead comes in, absolutely sucks. Do not reach out to them. Just mark them red. Right? Second one, not great, but fuck it. Let's see what happens on the call. Right?"   [09:30]  "Let's see if the person maybe ends up being a good lead. Third, orange, good lead, but it's not perfect, right? Still a good lead. I'm down to reach out to them. Like, they'll probably be a good client."   [09:42]  "And then fourth is like your perfect client, right? And so this is somebody that you guys understand and you know who's going to be your very perfect client that you have with experience have been able to look at right now."   [09:55]  "This is going to change quite often. I've changed my perfect client quite a bit what we're going to go after because as you start getting new clients, as you start to hop on some of these sales calls..."   [10:08]  "I had a month where I think I personally it was like two years ago, a year and a half ago, I took like 40 sales calls and they all fucking sucked. And I was like on sales calls. I was dropping fuck with clients."   [10:22]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Learning from Bad Calls"   NÚMEROS:  40 sales calls, $3K pricing   VISUAL:  Representação de frustração, necessidade de ajuste de critérios   [10:25]  "And I was like, man, like I was charging 3K at the time, too. And so I'm charging $3,000. And I couldn't even justify how to charge people because they had a really low ticket product. They had no volume to their store."   [10:40]  "So I had to go back to the drawing board and make sure now that the lead hunters knew exactly what we were looking for, right? So this is what you want to make sure that you grade them properly. It's really important."   [10:52]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lead Qualification Criteria"   NÚMEROS:  20,000-30,000 visitors minimum   VISUAL:  Lista de critérios - Website Quality, Product Uniqueness, Traffic, Ads   [10:58]  "So what we look for is number one, is the website built? Is it built well? Right? Number two, product. Is the product unique? If you guys are in e-commerce, when you have a really cool unique product, your brain starts flowing, right?"   [11:15]  "You're like, 'Oh, I could do this ad. I can do this. This is how I can show the product.' Right? That's what we look for. Then we look for website visits."   [11:25]  "Now, this has changed for me a bit. I used to not want to go after low ticket."   [11:30]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Case Study: Low Ticket Success"   NÚMEROS:  $40 planner, $1M+ monthly revenue   VISUAL:  Exemplo de produto de baixo ticket gerando alto volume   [11:35]  "I had a client recently who she sells like planners, right? She sells like these really like just normal planners for like moms and they're like $40 planner. She sells a bunch of like sticker books and stuff."   [11:48]  "And we ended up bringing her a million dollars a month just from Facebook ads in like last month, just like it was like the second month we started working with her. I was like, fuck. Okay."   [12:00]  "So, we've changed it now to be like, hey, if they have at least 20,000 30,000 visitors, low ticket, we'll take that, right? We'll have that sales call with that person real quick."   [12:12]  "Used to measure the visitors on the side? Oh, okay. So, that's good. Good question. So, Spyfu, you guys know Spyfu, right?"   [12:22]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Traffic Analysis Tool"   NÚMEROS:  20,000-30,000 organic visits   VISUAL:  Interface do Spyfu, métricas de tráfego orgânico vs pago   [12:25]  "So, you go to Spyfu, it'll tell you like the visitors, how many organic visits you get. So, we look at organic visits, not really paid, right?"   [12:35]  "Then we're going to look at ads. Do they have ads? If so, how good are they? Right? Do they use user generated, professional, just images? That's really important, but it's really that's like the last thing I really care about, honestly."   [12:50]  "If it's a good product, if it's unique, I don't give a fuck if they have ads to be honest with you. Like, I'll still go after somebody if they don't have any ads. Especially if they don't have ads and you can see that their product is amazing."   [13:05]  "They're like, 'Dude, this is going to be a sick client if we can get this person.' Right. Leads are transferred to a master sheet. Once they're past grading, now they go over to your video editor."   [13:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Video Editor Process"   NÚMEROS:  60 days custom pitches minimum   VISUAL:  Software Camtasia, processo de edição de vídeo   [13:25]  "Your video editor here, I record the general video pitch. So, if you guys are doing custom pitches, my recommendation here, pro tip is that don't create any custom or a general video pitch until you've done at least 60 days worth of custom video pitches."   [13:45]  "I did the mistake of creating a general video pitch in the very beginning here. And what happened to me was that I ended up bombing, right? I did it for like 90 days. I gave it to a VA. I said, 'Here's a general video pitch.' No calls were ever booked. It was an absolute shit show."   [14:05]  "So, what I did was I went back to the drawing board and I created custom video pitches. And what happens when you create the custom video pitches is that you're going to get so good at doing the custom video pitches that you're going to literally create a general video pitch every time you create a custom general video pitch, right?"   [14:25]  "Because you're going to say the same shit. Now, that's when you switch it over to general and you can actually scale."   [14:32]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Camtasia to YouTube Process"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Fluxo de trabalho - Camtasia → YouTube → Unlisted → Link   [14:38]  "Okay, you're done in Camtasia. When you do it in Camtasia, this allows you to upload it directly into YouTube. So the problem is a lot of people like if you're doing like Loom or you're doing another software most of them you can't just upload directly into YouTube where we actually send the link from YouTube in our pitch and then upload directly to YouTube and then create it unlisted in YouTube here."   [15:05]  "Yeah, you're good. Why do you want to upload it versus just sending the let's say... That's a good question. So I did it in the beginning because of Jeremy and at the time I think Loom wasn't very popular and so Loom was somewhat spammy, right?"   [15:22]  "People didn't know what Loom was. I still think that YouTube's more trustworthy. So when someone sees a YouTube link, they're willing to actually click it as opposed to a Loom link."   [15:35]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "YouTube Link Types"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Comparação youtube.be vs youtube.com, indicação de confiabilidade   [15:40]  "People aren't even hyperlinks are dangerous because it's still a hyperlink. They don't know who the fuck you are, right? So you want to make sure you use YouTube. And then there's two different types of YouTube links. This is actually something as well."   [15:55]  "Thanks for bringing this up because there's one where there's like YouTube.be, I think, or YouTube. Right? And then there's like YouTube.com. I found that YouTube.be looks spammy. You want to use like YouTube.com whatever it may be. So, make sure you don't use the other one."   [16:15]  "And it allows you to auto upload it. Then you can just grab the link right within Camtasia right there. So with YouTube, are you still able to have like an embed in the email way now? Like if you send a Loom video by Gmail, like you have that embed."   [16:32]  "You're talking about the popup of the video? Yeah. Yes. So that's what's actually done in Lemlist."   [16:42]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Loom Automation Limitation"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Restrição de embed do Loom com automação vs manual   [16:48]  "So what I did notice with Loom though is like you can only get that embed if you don't use automation. Really? I've tried it before because I wanted to do follow-ups with it and if you use automation, you can't do it."   [17:02]  "So it won't pop up. It has to be done through Gmail like automatically or like by yourself. Yeah. So it's a little rough. So this is like this makes it really easy and like the VAs can do everything for you basically." I'm talking about, hey, you know, I know we don't know each other. Same kind of structure that like Jeremy does."   [17:45]  "Dude, I take a lot of stuff obviously from Jeremy. This is not only mine. [18:15]  "So, you can be like, 'Hey, I noticed you're not spending much on Google or whatever.' Try to make it general, right? And you say like, 'Hey, I'm here and this is like what you guys are spending on Google.' And then you can go into like a Google account after that, right?" So, these are the three things that they actually screen record for me to put into the actual software. So, website screenshot, this is something else we actually have our VA do."   [18:50]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "imgbb.com - Custom Image Solution"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Processo de upload de screenshot para imgbb, geração de link customizado   [18:55]  "So, inside of Lemlist, and this is going to be a little bit later, you guys can like we'll go over. So, in order for us to pull a custom video image, so one of the things we found really hard was being able to give a custom video image for the prospect while also embedding a custom link in it without us being basically like doing it ourselves, right?"   [19:18]  "So, most softwares allow you to create a custom image, but not actually a custom link with a custom. You're basically custom on custom, right? So, that's the hardest part is custom on custom."   [19:30]  "So what we do is we take this screenshot and we upload it into a website called imgbb.com, imgbb.com. It gives you a link. Then that's how you're going to populate this custom image inside of the software Lemlist. So we'll go over it a little bit later. So make sure you save imgbb.com."   [19:52]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Sniper - Contact Hunter"   NÚMEROS:  Múltiplos contatos por empresa   VISUAL:  Hierarquia de contatos - Owners, Marketing Managers, Support   [19:58]  "So next is our sniper. So our sniper is the head hunter, right? He's going to find the contacts for each company. So what we want to find are the owners. So you want to find as many owners as you can. Make sure you find all the owners."   [20:15]  "Next thing you want to look for the marketing manager. Now, we don't ask them to find too many marketing manager emails or you're going to end up like having kind of like a bunch of shit emails."   [20:28]  "You want to have as many owner high leverage emails as you can because when you build this thing out in order to send you're going to have like 20 emails, separate emails if you have too many emails that you send out to."   [20:42]  "Standard support email really important as well. There are companies that will respond to the standard support email or standard support will forward it to the actual owner. So, you want to make sure that you go after the standard support email as well."   [20:58]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Contact Finding Tools"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Lista de ferramentas - snov.io, hunter.io, LinkedIn, SignalHire   [21:05]  "And in order to find these, I think we had a stack under this where it's not showing, but we use snov.io, snov.io. It actually links on their website. You click it, it'll give you a list of emails associated with that website when you go to it. Right."   [21:22]  "Have you found that's more accurate than hunter.io? We actually use something else besides Hunter, but Hunter is one of them. But we use multiple. Like we look at we look at LinkedIn, we look at Snov because they will all give you different results."   [21:38]  "So they're not all going to give you the same one. We have somebody that goes into like each one of them and tries to find like the best email that we can, right? Because if you only use Hunter, you only use Snov, you're not going to get all the results that you want, right?"   [21:55]  "So we'll go into LinkedIn, go into Snov, and we'll also go into... Man, it's on this. I totally forgot exactly what it was. Huh? No, it's not that. I'll try to give it to you guys later, but we go to another one and that's the one that we pay. I think we pay like 150 bucks a month for it or something and that's where like we find majority of our emails."   [22:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Contact Finding Stack"   NÚMEROS:  $150/month para SignalHire   VISUAL:  Três ferramentas principais - LinkedIn, snov.io, SignalHire   [22:25]  "Oh, there we go. Next slide. Perfect. LinkedIn, Snov, SignalHire. So, SignalHire is the one that we use."   [22:35]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Google Sheet Structure for Lemlist"   NÚMEROS:  11 campos obrigatórios   VISUAL:  Estrutura de planilha - Name, Email, Company, Website, Job Title, YouTube Link, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Lead Type, Screenshot Link   [22:40]  "So, from there, these are transferred to a separate Google sheet for Lemlist uploads. Now, in the sheet, you want to make sure you have name, email, company, website, job title, YouTube link, Facebook page, Instagram, LinkedIn, your lead type. This is really important in order to send these out to the right people, and then your screenshot link."   [23:05]  "So, go ahead. You can take a picture of this if you need to."   [23:12]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lemlist - The Core Platform"   NÚMEROS:  1,000 emails capability   VISUAL:  Logo/interface do Lemlist, destaque como ferramenta central   [23:18]  "All right. So, Lemlist. This is how I'm actually able to even send a thousand. Without Lemlist, I would not be able to send a thousand. There would be no way for me to do it. I'd have to have some type of other software."   [23:32]  "I actually for the longest time did this shit completely manual. I went I had HubSpot. I think Nick, I actually sent it to you in the beginning, right? And I had HubSpot and I would I had templates for HubSpot and I would click manually every day."   [23:50]  "There would be a task send email number two, send email number three, send email number four. And I did it automatically for like a fucking year because I couldn't figure out that custom on custom way to do this."   [24:05]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Why Lemlist?"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Lista de benefícios - Mass sending, Campaign creation, Email warming, Custom screenshots with hyperlinks   [24:12]  "So why Lemlist sends emails in mass quantities. You create your email campaigns. It warms up your email, which is really important. If you're going to start spamming people, you need to have a warm email. You need to have a lot of them."   [24:28]  "Can automatically show a custom screenshot for each company with a hyperlink to your custom video."   [24:35]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lemwarm - Email Warming Tool"   NÚMEROS:  2 weeks minimum warming period   VISUAL:  Duas partes do sistema - Lemlist + Lemwarm, processo de aquecimento   [24:42]  "There's two parts to Lemlist. There's Lemlist and there's what called Lemwarm. So Lemwarm is basically your warm-up tool, right? So Lemwarm is going to warm up all of your emails. You need to leave emails in here for at least two weeks."   [25:00]  "So if you go and you grab a new email and you just start bombarding people with it, it's going straight to spam and it's not going to land inbox and you guys are not going to be able to get any responses, right? It's going to go straight to spam."   [25:15]  "So again, Lemwarm warms up your email, gives you a deliverability score. So if you have a good score with Lemwarm, that's when you know you can start sending emails and then you can keep your emails warm all the time, right?"   [25:30]  "So when you have Lemwarm, you want to make sure that you just keep the emails warm. You do not want to stop Lemwarm because what this does basically is it like I believe what it does is it communicates between two people on the platform."   [25:45]  "Let's say I use Lemwarm, you use Lemwarm, they'll communicate together. You guys will literally send emails back and forth automatically. The platform will like send realistic emails and that's what gets past like the spam filters."   [26:02]  "So after two weeks you can actually you can start sending emails."   [26:08]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Multiple Email Setup Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  30-40 new emails per day maximum per domain   VISUAL:  Múltiplos domínios, cada um com seu próprio email   [26:15]  "So again you're going to want to set up multiple emails. So the way that you do this is you want to make sure that each email has its own domain. So it's not the email itself that gets spammed. It's actually the domain that gets spammed. Right?"   [26:32]  "So if I have for me Michael at motive marketing or I have info at motive marketing once motive marketing is fucked it's not going to work no matter what even though I put info in front of it a Michael it doesn't matter right so each email that you send from has to have its own domain."   [26:52]  "And you can only send 30 to 40 new emails a day which with each email and that includes basically not 30 to 40 companies 30 to 40 new emails so each company has anywhere between four to six different emails that you're reaching out to because of all of the owners."   [27:12]  "So you have to make sure that you have enough emails to have your mass quantity here. Takes approximately two weeks to warm up."   [27:22]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Domain Variation Examples"   NÚMEROS:  Múltiplas variações mostradas   VISUAL:  Lista de domínios - motive-marketing.com, motivemarketing.com, motivemarketng.com (sem i), motive-marketing.co, etc.   [27:28]  "So this is an example here. So my website motive-marketing.com and these are the types of domains that you want to create, right? So it's like motive marketing without the dash, motive-marketing.com, motive-marketing without an I, right?"   [27:45]  "So keep these all extremely relative to what you guys are like to your actual website, right? And if you use marketing in your actual website or your business is marketing obviously, you can just do exactly this."   [28:00]  "Look at this. Like you just take out an I, you take out an E, you add a co at the end of it. Like a bunch of little like finagle that you do in here. People have actually never said anything to me about the website, the domain being different in here than it actually is on my website."   [28:18]  "So regardless of the website that you're using for the domain, should you have your actual website within the email signature? Yep. Yep. Exactly."   [28:28]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Domain Forwarding Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Todos os domínios variados redirecionando para o website principal   [28:32]  "And then what you want to do too is all of the domains that you have, they need to forward to your website. So if you guys are like me, when anybody sends me a cold email or anything, I go check out the website the first thing, right?"   [28:48]  "So you want to make sure that if they do check out one of these domains, forward straight to your actual website."   [28:55]  "Does this does the same philosophy apply to subdomain? So I... So I believe a subdomain would be just same thing as a domain if it got spammed. I don't even know. Can you do subdomains as emails? Do you know?"   [29:12]  "Yeah, if you have mail... So I believe I'm not like an email specialist if you will, but I do believe that a subdomain if the domain gets like spam, the subdomain obviously is the same exact thing. So I think both of them will."   [29:28]  "So you want to make sure you have like literally completely different domains for everything." [30:12]  "Wait. So Okay. So if you're saying that it's not the email, it's the domain. So like if my like if I have like team I know you use like team app like motive at some point you fucking teach me this shit."   [30:28]  "So like if I have Nick asking you and I have like team app like when I warm up one am I warming up all of them essentially at the same time because they are the same domain. Warming up the domain. I'm not entirely sure."   [30:45]  "Okay. Yeah. Yeah. No, it would it wouldn't make sense. I mean, I wouldn't test it. You could. I would just use the email that you're warming up, right? But essentially, yeah, like if it if the domain all gets spammed, then it would essentially be the same thing."   [31:02]  "Can you warm up this Can you warm up two different emails that are on the same domain at the same time? Yeah, you could, but like I don't know if it would necessarily help cuz if one of them gets like spammed, you just lose two emails."   [31:18]  "Yeah, you just use one per domain. Yeah, exactly. You'd fuck up like... Yeah, you'd be like great. If you have a VA with an email, you would use a different domain for that VA."   [31:32]  "Yeah. [31:45]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Domain Lifespan & Tracking"   NÚMEROS:  20% open rate threshold, 2-6 months typical lifespan   VISUAL:  Gráfico de declínio de performance, linha de corte em 20%   [31:52]  "Have you seen even if you do everything right, it's only a matter of time for the domain to get burned. Yeah. Fucking yeah. Yeah. What's the timeline usually? It varies, man. Yeah, it varies."   [32:08]  "Like some of our emails get like spammed. Like you had it happen to you recently. G Suite will spam it and like you can still unspam it, but what we'll do is we track actually like every week and if we see one trending down and it doesn't trend back up and it hits like below 20%. We cut it and we literally just like get rid of that domain and we bring a new one in."   [32:32]  "Is it deliverability in Lemlist that you're seeing? Yes, but we actually track it manual as well. So you can see like how many emails have been like have been opened and everything."   [32:45]  "Lemlist data isn't as good. So, we'll still have my VA tracking and managed just like how many have been open and not. And then when open rates literally drop down below 20%. And we've seen like a decline over a couple weeks, I'll remove it."   [33:02]  "How are you getting around Apple's open rate like sabotage? I am not sure. No, I don't know. I'm not I'm basically just sending them out and hoping we we actually get the right stuff."   [33:18]  "Giving you as close to possible. Yeah. Yeah. Right. It may it may I may be getting wrong data now, right? But we did this before that happened and we haven't seen like any kind of change in our open rates."   [33:35]  "Like it didn't go from like we typically will see like good emails like new emails are going to be like 35 to like 50% open rates but we haven't seen it change like since that happened. Right. It didn't go from like 35 to now like 70, right? So it stayed about the same."   [33:52]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email Lifespan Statistics"   NÚMEROS:  2 months minimum, 6 months maximum typical, 35-50% open rates for good emails   VISUAL:  Timeline de vida útil do email, variação de performance   [34:00]  "So what do you what would you say is the typical lifeline? [34:18]  "And I've had some that like within two months were gone, right? So you just want to track it. [34:35]  "That's the most important part, right? Because if you don't want to wait two more weeks, you're going to lose 50 pitches a day, 20 pitches a day, whatever it may be. [38:05]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Lemlist Campaign Structure"   NÚMEROS:  7-10 different email campaigns, 7 emails per campaign, 2-3 days spacing   VISUAL:  Múltiplas campanhas paralelas, cada uma com sua sequência   [38:12]  "So I was going to go into Lemwarm but I don't think I'm going to have enough time here. So go to Lemlist. This is Lemlist builds your campaigns. Right. So, this is all your email campaigns."   [38:28]  "Each of your emails is going to need its own campaign, right? So, it's in its own campaign. You're going to have, you know, seven to 10 different emails inside of here because each one could reach out to 20 to 30 people."   [38:45]  "So, it's really important to have just a mass amount of emails in here, right? Each email needs a minimum of seven emails spread out two, three days apart, right?"   [39:00]  "We don't bombard people like every single day. There's like cold emails. We give them a couple days. I've noticed that it's just worked better if when I bombard people like every single day, you get more like, 'Leave me, leave me alone,' right? More like, 'Get the fuck out of here.'"   [39:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email Copy Best Practices"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Exemplos de emails curtos e diretos vs longos e spam-like   [39:25]  "Keep your emails very short and direct. This is obvious. I learned this from Jeremy. When you first start doing email outreach, you're like putting exclamation points and you're like trying to like be really happy and cool. It doesn't fucking work. You look like spam."   [39:42]  "Keep them really, really, really short and direct and just like, 'Hey, like the video. Hey, I made this for you, right? Let me know what you think.' Very short, direct like a real person though." [44:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email Spacing Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  2-3 days between emails, No emails on Sundays   VISUAL:  Calendário de envio, distribuição temporal   [44:25]  "Two to three days. So, like play around with it. Send one two days, one three days, couple two days. There's not really like an exact that I've like really found like you just want to kind of separate them a bit. We don't send emails on Sundays either. The only day we don't send anything."   [44:45]  "Also the emails stack up. So, if you have too many emails sending at once, you can end up going into spam as well."   [44:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Color-Coded Lead Tiers"   NÚMEROS:  3 tiers - Yellow (7 emails), Orange (7 + value), Green (7 + value + priority)   VISUAL:  Sistema de cores para classificação, diferentes estratégias por tier   [45:02]  "So the colors, right? So yellow, we have seven emails. These are the ones that were like, eh, fuck it. Let's just see if it works, right? Seven emails, we don't put any value follow-up in it."   [45:18]  "Orange, seven emails with a value follow-up. So value follow-up is like a week separated, right? But you can add value follow-up in your automated campaign sequence, which is really cool. So you have your value follow-ups in there. They can just send different kind of value to them. Same thing with green."   [45:38]  "So, one of the things with green emails, this is like going to be like your best email, the one that actually looks closest to your website domain. And your green one, you want to be really selective for who goes into like your perfect perfect client tier email because this is the one you don't want to go into spam."   [46:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Response Forwarding Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  10 sending emails → 1 response email   VISUAL:  Múltiplos emails de envio convergindo para um único email de resposta   [46:08]  "And what we actually do is we send all the emails, all the responses from every single email to this email, right? So, like instead of you're going to have like 10 different emails, right? Imagine your salesperson having to go between every single email, do the responses."   [46:28]  "When somebody actually writes back to us, it forwards to just one single email and you start the thread in conversation. Not only does it make it easier, what that actually does too is it allows you to continue to warm up that special email because you're having real conversation back and forth with somebody."   [46:48]  "Quick question. When you set up like emails and list and stuff, do you have to still set up like SPF records and records in the domain or do those tools do it for you?" So, you'll set that up and then you upload them into here."   [47:15]  "And then Jay, do I got any time to jump into like a live Lemlist here? I got it looks like 12 seconds. Huh? [53:15]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Quality Control Process"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Checagem visual antes de envio, verificação de imgbb links   [53:22]  "So what you want to make sure you do is that... If it doesn't work, it's going to show right here and all the emails in this sequence are screwed up, right?"   [53:45]  "So you always want to check once this one's working. You can see you now we're just want to click like this. And now we just sent a pitch, right? So bam, we sent a pitch. Next one looks good. We send the next pitch. Looks good. Send the next pitch, right? They all look good. Send the pitch. Bam. We just sent five pitches."   [54:08]  "You're sending... You're sending these one by one, but then they're all just added to that sequence. Yep. Campaign is a sequence. Yep. Exactly. And you don't have to actually check them like that."   [54:25]  "What I did notice is when you use a software like the imgbb, sometimes it won't pull up. So, we have our VA check it. I don't know why."   [54:35]  "How do you connect that imgbb to the campaign? Is it already like linked? Is there like some kind of fucking connection already? Yeah, good question."   [54:48]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "imgbb Integration Method"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificado   VISUAL:  Fluxo - Google Sheet cell com link imgbb → Lemlist custom variable → Email template   [54:55]  "So, so what that's going to look like is inside of the spreadsheet that you upload into Lemlist, there's a custom variable there that connects to Lemlist and that connects to it. Yeah."   [55:10]  "So, the link is in the spreadsheet in a cell that... Exactly. And that uploads into here and then the way that you've built out that actual video in there. That's how it's going to it's going to pull it in. Right."   [55:25]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Multiple Campaign Overview"   NÚMEROS:  20-30 pitches per email per day   VISUAL:  Lista de múltiplas campanhas ativas, cada uma enviando em paralelo   [55:32]  "So, that's kind of like quick overview. You can see every single one of them. They're just different ones here. I don't have any in here because we're not we didn't send any, but all of our emails are just different. Right?"   [55:48]  "Every single one of these are sending anywhere between 20 to 30 different pitches pretty much every single day, right? You can only send up to 40. I used I tried to push the envelope and send like 45 50. I was like, I just don't want to have so many emails in here. I got spammed immediately."   [56:10]  "So, you want to stick to like 30 or 40 max in order to be able to not like spam your email out and it's gone right away."   [56:22]  "That's per domain. Per domain. Exactly. So every single one of these that it actually sends from is one domain. There's not multiple domains and multiple campaigns. It's one domain for every single campaign inside of here."   [56:40]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email-Only Outreach Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  1,000 pitches via email   VISUAL:  Foco exclusivo em email, não social media   [56:48]  "So you're only sending your video pictures through email. Yeah. Social. So I only send them through email because of the mass quantity that I can send."   [57:00]  "And I noticed like when I we pitch ecom, right? So like ecom is a fucked up industry that gets pitched like crazy. And I noticed that I don't really get responses via Instagram too much from ecom. It's almost always here and it's a fucking volume game, dude."   [57:20]  "Like you have to catch people at the right time. You have to pitch just a shitload of people, right? Most of them will tell you like, 'Dude, like any like you'll get a lot of response, I'm sure you do, too, of people that say like you should see how many times I get pitched, but this was pretty good.' And so like I responded, right?"   [57:40]  "They're like, 'Dude, I get 10 of these a day.' So just a volume game and you can't do volume through Instagram, right? And I don't get good responses anyway. I usually get them here."   [57:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email Performance Analysis Question"   NÚMEROS:  Não rastreado especificamente   VISUAL:  Discussão sobre qual email gera mais responses/appointments/sales   [58:02]  "Have you done like worked your way back to figure out like which email in the sequence ends up getting you one like the most responses to like the most actual appointments and then three the actual most amount of sales."   [58:18]  "That's a good question. I actually have not. I should but it's something that we'd have to do manually. It's a lot of work. I'm really curious."   [58:30]  "No, I haven't, man. I just have done this and it's worked really well and they go through like all seven of them."   [58:38]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email Response Patterns"   NÚMEROS:  First 7 emails = most responses, Value follow-ups = fewer responses   VISUAL:  Padrão de resposta por posição na sequência   [58:45]  "The one thing I have noticed here too is that the first seven emails they respond the most from value follow-up, they don't respond as much. So they don't respond typically in the first seven. That's why in our yellow one, we only have seven."   [59:00]  "Like if they don't respond that first seven, they're more likely not going to respond. And they respond most to the very first one, the second one, and the very last one that says like, 'Hey, am I annoying you?'"   [59:18]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Performance Metrics"   NÚMEROS:  35-50% open rates (good emails), 20% minimum threshold, 3% booking rate, 80% show rate, 16-20% response rate   VISUAL:  Estatísticas de performance do sistema completo   [59:25]  "What would you say like open response and like call percentages are that you're getting roughly? Yeah. So, our open rates hover between like like I told you on the low end if they go below like 20, but we're probably around like our good emails 35 to 50% in open rates and that's manually tracked basically like we look at how many people open inside this software and then we go like put it against how many we've sent."   [59:55]  "But booked calls we only get 3% of total companies into booking a call. And now I think yeah I think with different... No, like I used to do it like I did with personal brands not here and we used to get like 20 25%. Right. So..."   [1:00:15]  "So I believe it's like industry specific, you know, like because of e-commerce. I believe that like that's why it's so low because we've tried it like I pitched like turf companies before like like like astro turf like turf you get into you know you get your yard right and we got a way more like a higher like booking rate you know and when we do personal brands it's higher so like..."   [1:00:40]  "No one's pitching them too like that. Exactly. Right. But but ecom they get pitched like fucking crazy, right? So we only get like a 3%. So that's why like you have to have volume. So you can imagine if you have an industry that responds quite a bit, you can easily get more than 3%. With this..."   [1:01:00]  "My response probably about like 16 to 20% something like that. You get a lot of fuck you know like a lot of people are just like dude stop leave me alone. But like that's fine. I'll take it. And you have to just be like you could you just have to not care what people write back because people will just talk so you just people talk mad shit."   [1:01:22]  "It's hilarious. Yeah. People talk a hell of shit. It's kind of fun. Yeah."   [1:01:30]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Conversion Funnel Summary"   NÚMEROS:  3% book calls → 80% show rate   VISUAL:  Funil de conversão do pitch ao call   [1:01:38]  "3% of people companies you pitch end up getting on call. Mhm. Yeah. So they book a call and then we get like 80% show rate or something like that. And we don't even really use like any marketing sequences or anything. It's just like most of these companies end up hopping on the call."   [1:01:58]  "Yeah. It's more like, you know, they took the time to watch the video. Like, it works."   [1:02:05]  "Anybody else? Any questions? I think I got a cut off."   [1:02:12]  "Have you been keeping track of video watch time? No, I don't keep track of watch times. I basically just like mad volume, dude. Right."   [1:02:25]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Post-Sequence Strategy"   NÚMEROS:  7 emails = done, Good leads = value follow-up   VISUAL:  Fim da sequência, bifurcação entre leads bons e regulares   [1:02:32]  "After the sequence, what happens? They're done. They're out. The good leads, I put them in value follow-up, right? And they get and this is this just this is literally a volume game, dude. Right."   [1:02:48]  "So, like I know like Jay Jay Jay does a good job and Leo does a good job of like the value sequence those like for the personal brands like with these dude it's like there's so many e-commerce stores I don't waste my time with any type of value really like I just like seven they don't answer done right the next ones and your little VA army will be able to just spit out thousands of leads for you know."   [1:03:15]  "We good anybody else? Give it up for Michael."     📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO   NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS: 1,000 video pitches por mês 4 VAs + 1 Salesperson = estrutura completa 2-3 VAs hunters 200, 500, 1,000 pitches/mês (níveis mencionados) $3K pricing inicial mencionado $40 produto (case planner) $1M+ por mês em revenue (case cliente planner) 20,000-30,000 visitors mínimo para low ticket $150/mês para SignalHire 30-40 emails novos por dia máximo por domínio 2 semanas mínimo para warming de emails 4 níveis de grading (Red, Yellow, Orange, Green) 7 emails por sequência mínimo 2-3 dias entre emails 35-50% open rates (emails bons) 20% open rate = threshold de corte 3% booking rate (e-commerce) 20-25% booking rate (personal brands) 80% show rate 16-20% response rate 2-6 meses lifespan típico de domínio 40 sales calls (exemplo de mês ruim) 60 dias mínimo de custom pitches antes de general 90 dias teste inicial (falhou) 11 campos obrigatórios na Google Sheet 7-10 diferentes campanhas de email 4-6 emails diferentes por company $50K estimado para oferecer como serviço   ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:   Sistema de 5 Passos: Hunters (encontram leads) Lead Grader (qualifica leads) Video Editor (edita vídeos em Camtasia) Sniper (encontra contatos) Lemlist + Salesperson (automação e fechamento)   Lead Sources: Google Amazon Instagram (personal brands) storeleads.com (e-commerce)   Contact Finding Stack: snov.io hunter.io LinkedIn SignalHire ($150/mês)   Lead Grading System (4 cores): Red: Não contactar Yellow: Não ótimo, mas testar (7 emails sem value follow-up) Orange: Bom lead (7 emails + value follow-up) Green: Cliente perfeito (7 emails + value follow-up + prioridade máxima)   Critérios de Qualificação: Website bem construído Produto único Traffic (20K-30K+ visitors) Ads (qualidade, tipo)   7-Email Sequence: Introdução do vídeo Cliff Notes (resumo bullet points) Strategy showcase (funnalytics diagram) Results/Testimonials Meme (Conor McGregor) "Did you see this?" (1 semana depois) "Don't want to annoy you"   Domain Strategy: 1 domínio por email Variações do domínio principal (sem letras, .co, com hífen, etc.) Todos os domínios forward para website principal SPF records setup obrigatório   Warming Strategy: Lemwarm por 2 semanas mínimo Monitoramento semanal de performance Corte abaixo de 20% open rate Emails extras sempre warming como backup   Video Pitch Components: General pitch gravado VA screen records: website, ad library, Spyfu Upload para YouTube (unlisted) Usar youtube.com (não youtube.be) Screenshot via imgbb.com para custom image   Lemlist Features: Mass email sending Email warming (Lemwarm) Custom screenshots com hyperlinks customizados Integração HubSpot Variáveis em imagens ({{firstName}}, {{companyName}}) Automated follow-up sequences Auto-remove após resposta   Response Management: Todos os responses forward para 1 email verde Salesperson assume após resposta Mantém email verde warm com conversas reais   ESTRUTURA:   Team Structure: 2-3 Hunter VAs (online jobs.ph) 1 Lead Grader (salesperson ou marketer atual) 1 Video Editor VA (Camtasia) 1 Sniper VA (contact finder) 1 Salesperson 1 OG VA que recruta outros (3 anos, família toda no staff)   Google Sheet Structure: Name Email Company Website Job Title YouTube Link Facebook Page Instagram LinkedIn Lead Type Screenshot Link   Contact Hierarchy: Owners (prioridade máxima) Marketing Managers (seletivo) Standard Support Email   Software Stack: Camtasia (video editing) YouTube (hosting) Lemlist (email automation) Lemwarm (email warming) imgbb.com (image hosting) Spyfu (traffic analysis) snov.io (email finding) hunter.io (email finding) SignalHire (email finding - $150/mês) HubSpot (CRM integration) G Suite (email) Online Jobs PH (VA recruitment)   DADOS FINANCEIROS: Investimento SignalHire: $150/mês Pricing inicial mencionado: $3K Case de sucesso: $1M+/mês (cliente planner $40) ROI potencial como serviço: $50K setup   DURAÇÃO:  ~1:03:15  FORMATO:  Apresentação ao vivo com demonstração de software e Q&A
